$schema: hand-written deck-word entries, merged over the generated deck (see lib/words.js)
$forms: { id, remove: true }           drop a generated word
{ simp, pinyinNum, band }      add a word, taking defs and trad from CC-CEDICT
{ simp, pinyinNum, band, defs } add a word CC-CEDICT does not have
$notes: Merged after HSK resolution so overrides win, but before sentences and stroke data,
so an added word is finished exactly like a generated one.
Adding a word here mints a permanent card id — change `simp`/`pinyinNum` of an
existing entry only if you accept that its review history is orphaned.
$declinedNote: Homograph candidates reviewed and deliberately NOT split. report.txt prints these
as DECLINED with the reason instead of re-raising them as candidates every build.
Remove a spelling from here to have it surface as a candidate again.

declinedSplits.会: The only untaught reading is kuài, a bound form living almost entirely in 会计 (accounting). Band 3 会2 is the noun sense 'meeting; gathering' (开会, 会议) — still huì. Splitting would put kuài on a card that means meeting.
declinedSplits.和: Untaught readings are huò (to mix), hè (to reply in verse) and huó (to knead flour). Band 7 和2 is ambiguous across all three, and may well still be hé in a further sense. Three plausible candidates and no way to choose.
declinedSplits.喂: Readings are wèi (to feed; hey) and wéi (hello, on the phone). CC-CEDICT's wèi entry already covers 'hey', and the phone greeting is pronounced wèi by most speakers. Two cards learners cannot tell apart by sound.
declinedSplits.乘: The untaught reading shèng is glossed '(archaic) four-horse military chariot' — a classical measure word, not band-6 vocabulary. Band 6 乘2 is far more likely chéng ('to multiply', 乘法; 'to take advantage of', 乘机), which is the reading already taught.

$seedNote: The bootstrap vocabulary (Phase 7 §2): the words no example sentence can
precede, introduced in this order before the n+1 pass takes over. Kept here
rather than in code because it is curation, not logic.

words.$group: HSK 3.0 words absent from CC-CEDICT. Definitions hand-written — review welcome.

defs: to be the highlight of a program

words.$group: Curated homograph splits. The HSK marker (别1/别2) separates senses, and only sometimes readings — see the homograph section of report.txt for every marked entry and its candidates. Only splits with a genuinely distinct reading belong here; defs come from CC-CEDICT.
